results.title: Bloka: AI turns any object into your focus trigger | Product Hunt
results.content:
Luca Flati
Bloka

Bloka

Dmitry Petrakov

@luca\_flati Love the idea of turning focus into a physical ritual instead of a setting.

Curious if you’ve noticed a difference between blocking distractions and making people more aware of what they’re about to do next.

It feels like the object isn’t just a lock, but a moment of intentional pause – which might be the real behavior change here.

Luca Flati
Bloka

Bloka

@dmitry\_petrakov That’s a great distinction. We’ve found that the 'physicality' of Bloka turns focus into a commitment. It's less about 'I'm not allowed to use my phone' and more about 'I have chosen to do this task.' Have you personally found physical cues more effective than digital ones in your own workflow?

Dmitry Petrakov [...] The wedding ring example is powerful – it makes the ritual less about control and more about identity. That feels like a very different (and more sustainable) behavior change lever.

Luca Flati
Bloka

Bloka

Louis Chen
PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro 

PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro

@luca\_flati Thanks, glad that was helpful!  
Totally agree — subtle haptics as the anchor, with visuals as gentle confirmation feels just right.  
I love that you’re experimenting with different patterns; it’s interesting how small changes in haptic feedback can create very different “state change” experiences. I'm excited to see how this evolves and how users respond!

Jennifer Klepper
Asterix Writer

Asterix Writer [...] Louis Chen
PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro 

PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro

@luca\_flati I really like how this leans into behavior design instead of just stronger blocking.  
The ritual aspect feels more important than the restriction itself.

Did you experiment with adding any visual or haptic feedback to reinforce that moment of “I’m entering focus mode”?

Luca Flati
Bloka

Bloka

Louis Chen
PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro 

PurrrrrFocus Pomodoro

@luca\_flati That’s a great question.  
For me, tactile feedback tends to create a stronger immediate anchor — it’s harder to ignore, and it marks a clear “state change.”  
Visual cues are great for context, but I often mentally tune them out if they’re too subtle or too familiar.

results.title: BePresent Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
### Standout features

 ✓Daily challenges for behavior change
 ✓Milestones and progress-based coaching
 ✓Simple, low-friction UX
 ✓Accountability to stay consistent
 ✓Habit-focused approach beyond time limits

## clearspace

clearspaceclearspace

make your phone less addicting

4.8 · 15 reviews

Learn more →

Clearspace is built around a micro-pause that interrupts impulse app opens before they become a session. Rather than motivating through gamification like BePresent, it changes the moment of decision by adding just enough friction to make usage intentional. [...] 4.2 · 17 reviews

Learn more →

If the goal is deeper behavior change than phone-only screen-time gamification, Focus Bear leans into full-day structure: routines, focus blocks, and breaks that shape how work actually happens. It’s designed to reduce context switching and procrastination by adding guardrails across both mobile and desktop, which is a meaningful step up from solutions that mainly influence phone usage. [...] Its challenge format encourages small daily wins that build awareness of triggers and routines, helping users step out of doomscrolling cycles without needing heavy-handed blocking. The experience tends to feel like a lightweight program: doable actions, clear milestones, and a sense of progress that’s driven by behavior change rather than just time caps.

Zario also emphasizes simplicity, which helps maintain consistency—especially for people who abandon tools that feel complex or punitive. If the primary need is unbreakable enforcement, a friction or blocking tool may be stronger; but for sustainable habit change with gentle accountability, Zario is a great fit.

### Best for

Best for users who want an AI-coach style program with simple daily challenges to reduce doomscrolling.

results.title: Grain: Talk to your analytics. Understand every user with AI. | Product Hunt
results.content:
• Overview (live): A real-time dashboard where new events stream in. See starters, converters, core metrics such as MAUs and new users, plus top events and trends.

• Built-in Database View: Query your raw events and curated views in a tenant-scoped database view. Filter by events, custom properties, timeframes, and more using our visual query builder.  
  
Tracks is that view. It reconstructs journeys from any start to any goal and surfaces the moments that matter—dominant paths, the hubs where traffic concentrates, and last steps before drop-off. When you see something, you don’t have to ship code to test an update: remote config is built in, so you can flip variants and typed variables from the dashboard and watch the change land immediately in clients.  
  
Why it’s different [...] Promoted

Eray Ocak

EventConnect

EventConnect

Maker

📌

Hello everyone 👋  
  
I'm Eray, founder of Grain ♦️♦️  
  
We built Grain because funnels don’t tell the whole story. Real users zig-zag. They loop, hesitate, bounce, and return. We wanted a way to see actual paths in real time—then change app behavior without a deploy.  
  
What Grain is

• Tracks (real-time journeys): Map how users move from a start event to a goal. See paths, hubs (important steps), and the last step before drop-off.

• Remote Config: Flip variants and typed variables from the dashboard to tune UX—no redeploys.

• Fast Ingestion (SDK + REST): Lightweight web SDK with automatic batching/retries, and consent-aware delivery (no non-essential storage pre-consent in the EU/UK/TR). [...] Subscribe
